judgments.comparative_verdict: Report 2 is slightly better: both nail (a), (b), and (c), but only Report 2 recommends adding a global/environment-level gate (d).

report_1.evidence: Reminders **are sent automatically**. The decision is made in `auto_send_reminders_enabled` at `backend/app/feature_flags.py:10-13`... `.get(...)` returns `None`, the `is None` check on line 11 is true, and line 12 returns `True`.

report_2.evidence: Reminders **are sent automatically** by default. The deciding code is `auto_send_reminders_enabled` in `slotwise/backend/app/feature_flags.py:10-13`... `.get(...)` returns `None`, and the function returns `True`.

anything that triggers outbound messaging on a user's behalf should invert to default-off when null/missing... I would change the default at `slotwise/backend/app/feature_flags.py:11-12`, inverting it to default-off

report_1.evidence: `compact_calendar_view_enabled` (the function this was copied from) controls a cosmetic UI preference — defaulting it "on" for everyone is low-risk and easily reversible. `auto_send_reminders_enabled` controls whether the system contacts real patients by SMS/email... the two settings have very different blast radii (a UI layout preference vs. outbound communication to third parties)

report_2.evidence: `compact_calendar_view_enabled` was introduced first... a genuine UI-preference/convenience flag for which default-on is reasonable. Then commit `aa4cf8d`... added `auto_send_reminders_enabled` directly beneath it, with an identical `is None: return True` body — a mechanical copy-paste... a convenience-flag pattern applied unmodified to an outbound-messaging flag.

Add an env-level master switch (per project documentation's "Rollout flags" section) ANDed with the per-tenant value, so the feature can be enabled per-environment... and so there's a single kill switch to disable all automated sends fleet-wide if something goes wrong — right now there is no such switch anywhere in the repo
